lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- Small Talk
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Pricila Adamo | Aula 11 -- Small Talk

listenings.text: Hi there. Lovely weather today, isn't it? I noticed you're reading a guidebook. Are you a tourist here too? I'm from Canada, and this is my first time in this city. It's quite beautiful, isn't it? I love how friendly the locals are. Funny coincidence, I sat next to a Brazilian on the plane yesterday. Small world, right? Anyway, I should let you get back to your book. It was nice chatting with you. Enjoy your trip.

I used to be terribly shy with strangers. On my first trips abroad, I would just smile and look away. But over the years I learned that a little chat goes a long way. Now, when I'm waiting in line or sitting in a cafe, I often break the ice with a simple comment about the weather or the place. People are usually kind, and sometimes a short conversation turns into a wonderful memory. You don't need perfect English to connect with someone. You just need the courage to say hello.

extra_audio.text: First, a stranger smiles and comments on the lovely weather. Then he asks if Pricila is a tourist as well. Next, Pricila says she is from Brazil and they discover a coincidence. After that, they chat about the friendly locals and the beautiful city. Finally, they wish each other a nice trip and say goodbye.
